heading: Contributions of comfort and other incidental items and services during emergency duty
label: 1972 Contributions of comfort and other incidental items and services during emergency duty
text: In addition to the authority provided under , at any time on or after , the Capitol Police Board may accept contributions of comfort and other incidental items and services to support officers and employees of the United States Capitol Police while such officers and employees are on duty in response to emergencies involving the safety of human life or the protection of property.section 1971 of this titleJanuary 10, 2002
notes: CodificationSection was classified to section 206d–1 of former Title 40, prior to the enactment of Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, by , , .Pub. L. 107–217, § 1116 Stat. 1062Aug. 21, 2002
